channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: 'My soul left my body': customers see bills in the billions after AWS billing system goes haywire — but don't go emptying your bank accounts
item.description: An Amazon AWS billing system accidentally told some customers they owed billions of dollars, but a fix is on the way.

item.title: 'You're giving ballistic ⁠missiles to individuals with Mythos': JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon says Anthropic's AI model poses some serious risks
item.description: Restricting Mythos to vetted organizations and keeping it out of the hands of the public seems to be the safest option for Anthropic, with JPMorgan CEO describing its risks as a “real issue.”

item.title: 'Superb for most home uses in an emergency': Jackery HomePower 3000 combines a huge 3,072Wh battery with enough power to run almost every appliance — and it's on sale right now
item.description: It combines a powerful 3,600W output with fast charging and solar compatibility.

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5000 Super GPUs rumored to be ready — but they're on hold, and the reason why makes me nervous about pricing
item.description: These Super refreshes are still coming, by all accounts, but they could make quite the dent in your bank balance.

item.title: Capital One releases VulnHunter, an open-source AI tool that finds software flaws before hackers do
item.description: Capital One on Thursday released VulnHunter, an open-source, agentic AI security tool that scans source code for exploitable vulnerabilities, maps out how an attacker would reach them, and proposes targeted fixes — all before a single line ships to production. The tool, built internally and now available on GitHub under an Apache 2.0 license, is one of the most ambitious attempts by a major financial institution to turn offensive AI capabilities into a public defensive resource.The move marks a striking philosophical turn for a company still defined, in many boardrooms, by a 2019 data breach t...

item.title: Meta is reportedly considering a multibillion-dollar data center deal with Anthropic
item.description: It would open up a completely new business for the Facebook and Instagram maker.

item.title: Intuit scrapped its own AI agent architecture twice in four months. At VB Transform 2026, its AI VP called that the fast path
item.description: Intuit was an early pioneer in the usage of agentic AI, but its path to success has hardly been a straight line.At VB Transform 2026, Intuit VP of AI Nhung Ho described how the company rebuilt its agent architecture twice in the span of about four months, first moving from a fleet of specialist agents to a central orchestration layer, then abandoning that layer for a skills and tools based system once the orchestrator itself started failing under its own complexity. The full second rebuild took 60 days, with a first working version in under 20.The failure mode that forced the second rewrite wa...

item.title: Meta’s AI bots drain publisher pockets with 9 billion Q2 2026 requests at host expense while returning ZERO traffic — as ChatGPT claims 88% of AI referrals
item.description: While ChatGPT remains the leader in AI traffic referrals, Meta AI bot activity has grown considerably, and now dominates.

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

item.title: Apple Music augmente ses prix en France : jusqu’à 3 euros de plus par mois selon les formules
item.description: Les services Apple sont aussi touchés par l'inflation générale à Cupertino. Quelques semaines après les Macbook et iPad, c'est au tour d'Apple Music et Apple One de voir leur grille tarifaire changer.

item.title: Shark’s versatile ChillPill cooling system is back to its best price
item.description: Portable fans are one of the easiest ways to stay cool during the summer, and you don’t have to spend much to find a decent one. If you’re looking for something more versatile, though, Shark’s ChillPill Personal Fan and Cooling System combines a rechargeable fan with a dry-touch evaporative mister in a design that can […]

item.title: The viral watermelon ice cream hack looks like magic. Does it actually work?
item.description: All you need is a watermelon, milk or cream and about five minutes of scraping to try the frozen dessert filling up social media feeds. For anyone looking to cool off this summer, a new viral recipe is transforming one of the season’s most iconic fruits into a creamy frozen dessert, no ice cream maker required.

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

item.title: The Samsung S99H is one of the best OLED TVs I've ever tested, even if it does feature a controversial design
item.description: Samsung's latest flagship OLED TV, the S99H, delivers on picture and performance, but features a controversial design change.

item.title: The Samsung S95H is one of the best OLED TVs I've ever tested, even if it does feature a controversial design
item.description: Samsung's latest flagship OLED TV, the S95H, delivers on picture and performance, but features a controversial design change.

item.title: Where is Gemini 3.5 Pro? The AI model announced at Google I/O is still MIA.
item.description: Google didn't launch Gemini 3.5 Pro at Google I/O. Now, the company has blown past its promised launch date.
